ocr.text: The Licensing and Partnership Deals table for Merck & Co, encompassing the Pharmaceuticals and Biotech sectors, provides a
comprehensive overview of key financial metrics over a five-year compound annual growth rate (CAGR) period, denominated
in USD. The first financial metric under consideration is the 5-Year CAGR, which shows a progressive increase across the
years: in 2021, the 5-Year CAGR stood at 5.6%, rising to 6.2% in 2022, and further increasing to 6.8% in 2023.
Correspondingly, the price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio also exhibits a positive trend, beginning at 15.2 in 2021, advancing to 16.0 in
2022, and reaching 16.5 by 2023. The current ratio, indicative of liquidity and short-term financial health, reflects an upward
trajectory as well, with values of 1.8 in 2021, 1.9 in 2022, and 2.0 in 2023, demonstrating an improvement in the company’s

ability to cover its short-term liabilities. 

Examining liquidity ratios, the debt-to-equity ratio is crucial for understanding Merck's
leverage, which was recorded at 60% in 2021, increasing to 62% in 2022, and further escalating to 64% in 2023, indicating a
growing reliance on debt financing over this period. The gross margin, a measure of production efficiency and profitability, was
noted at 32% in 2021, with an increase to 34% in 2022, and a subsequent rise to 36% in 2023, revealing enhanced operational
efficiency and cost management.
FinancialMetrics  5-YearCAGR  P/ERatio  CurrentRatio
LiquidityRatios  Debt/Equity  GrossMargin  OperatingMargin  NetProfitMargin
The operating margin, crucial for assessing Merck’s core profitability, showed consistent strength, starting at 15.2 in 2021,
moving to 16.0 in 2022, and 16.5 in 2023, which aligns with the overall growth in profitability metrics. Lastly, the net profit
margin, which reflects the percentage of revenue that remains as profit after all expenses, was established at 60% in 2021,
increasing to 62% in 2022, and culminating at 64% in 2023, thus demonstrating a robust financial performance and an effective

strategy in maintaining profitability amidst operational costs.

In summary, the Licensing and Partnership Deals table details
significant financial metrics for Merck & Co over the specified five-year CAGR period, illustrating a consistent upward
movement across all metrics. The 5-Year CAGR increased from 5.6% to 6.8%, the P/E ratio improved from 15.2 to 16.5, and
the current ratio enhanced from 1.8 to 2.0, all indicative of strengthened financial health and operational efficiency.
Furthermore, liquidity ratios show an increased debt-to-equity ratio from 60% to 64%, while the gross margin rose from 32% to
36%, alongside improvements in operating and net profit margins, from 15.2 to 16.5 and 60% to 64%, respectively. This
comprehensive data underscores Merck's strategic direction and operational effectiveness within the pharmaceuticals and
biotech sectors, reflecting a strong commitment to growth and sustainability in financial performance over the observed period.
